reviews.review_text: Mortal Kombat X is a fast, brutal fighting game with great combat and awesome fatalities. Fun to play, but story is short and PC performance can be a bit rough.

MKX is very light to load even for mid-low PC and also pretty much fun to play, definitely worth buying.
Just started the Mortal Kombat franchise with this game. An incredible story with  plenty of choices for  characters with unique mechanics and moves. A must play fighting game.
Cool game and cool story but combat sometimes feels frustating for me in story mode (I use keyboard control, so yeah maybe that's why)

The game is good but not that much, the achievements are boring and poorly tracked. The crypt was somewhat unexpected, but empty. I liked the story. 7.5/10 maybe?

I'm horrible at fighters, but this is probably my favorite modern MK game.  I play single player found it easy to jump in.  The gameplay is quick and there is less of a grind to unlock stuff like the later installments.  Some complain that it is dark, but i feel it is appropriate to the tone of the game.  It reminds me of MK2 a lot.

The best Mortal Kombat game experience, similar fighting style as Ultimate Mortal Kombat 3. A masterpiece in depth, combos, movement, horror movie guest characters, interactable stage brutalities, variations. A timeless game, celebrated in tournaments even after 11 years of its release. Players coming from other newer mortal kombat games to MKX are hooked instantly with the freedom of play style, a healthy community, and random players that can turn to true life friends in just a few matches of playing and sharing character knowledge. 

10/10

Mortal Kombat X features a good story mode that kept me entertained from beginning to end. Among the new generation of fighters, Cassie Cage stood out as my favorite thanks to her fun and enjoyable gameplay style.

The combat system felt fast and dynamic, which I preferred over the slower pace of previous entries. I completed the story mode in one sitting because I was so engaged with the experience. The graphics and atmosphere were also impressive, helping make the game feel intense and visually striking throughout.

Mortal Kombat X is really fun to play and enjoyable, especially when fighting against different opponents. The story mode is one of the best parts of the game and keeps you engaged from start to finish.

If you're a beginner, I recommend going to the Options menu and changing the difficulty from Medium to Easy. Even then, learning combos can be challenging and takes some practice.

Overall, Mortal Kombat X is definitely worth playing and offers hours of entertainment.
Easily the best mk gameplay out and the last mk that has the running mechanic!
VERY fun game to play with friends or not. buttt the skill level is too high for some new players

I genuinely think this is one of the best Mortal Kombat games ever made. I haven’t played Mortal Kombat 11 or Mortal Kombat 1 yet, but after spending a lot of time with Mortal Kombat X, I can confidently say this game still feels amazing even years later.

The gameplay is fast, aggressive, and incredibly fun. Every character feels unique thanks to the variation system, which gives different playstyles for the same fighter. Whether you play casually with friends or want to learn combos seriously, the game stays entertaining for a long time.

One thing I personally love the most is The Krypt mode. Compared to previous Mortal Kombat games, The Krypt here feels much more alive, immersive, and memorable. Exploring the island, unlocking secrets, finding fatalities, costumes, and hidden content actually feels rewarding instead of just being a simple menu. It adds a sense of adventure outside the fighting itself, and honestly became one of my favorite parts of the game.

The story mode is also very cinematic and easy to enjoy even if you are not deeply into Mortal Kombat lore. The graphics still hold up surprisingly well, and the soundtrack plus sound effects make every fight feel intense.

Of course, the game isn’t perfect. Online matchmaking can sometimes be inconsistent depending on your region and platform activity today. But if you enjoy fighting games, especially fast-paced and brutal ones, this game is absolutely worth trying.

Overall, I highly recommend Mortal Kombat X to both newcomers and longtime fans. Even after all these years, it still delivers one of the most fun and satisfying Mortal Kombat experiences.

#d'vorahsucks

Mortal Kombat X is the only game where I can say “I’m relaxing after a stressful day” while actively watching someone get turned inside out by a four-armed monster. And somehow...It’s therapeutic.

Mortal Kombat X is absolute chaos in the best possible way. The gameplay is fast, brutal, and aggressively disrespectful. Every match feels like a personal grudge match between two people who absolutely refuse to let the other one have happiness. The roster is amazing too. You’ve got ninjas, demons, cyborgs, movie monsters, and at least three people whose main hobby seems to be spinal cord removal. Beautiful diversity :) 
And can we talk about the X-Ray attacks for a second? Nothing humbles you faster than watching your favourite character’s entire skeleton explode in 4K because you got too confident button mashing. The game also has that perfect balance between: “I’m actually getting pretty good at this.” and getting hit by a 37-hit combo so disrespectful you physically put the controller down and stare at the wall for a minute.

The story mode is basically an action movie fueled by caffeine and unresolved anger, and I mean that lovingly. Every character speaks like they’re moments away from committing a felony. Also, the online experience? Incredible. Nothing builds character faster than being absolutely annihilated by someone named something like “xX420NoobDestroyerXx” who has clearly not seen sunlight since launch day.

But genuinely, this game is iconic. The combat feels amazing, the visuals still hold up ridiculously well, and the sheer over-the-top violence somehow loops back around into comedy. It knows exactly what it is and commits to the bit harder than almost any fighting game ever made.

10/10.
Lost several matches.
Lost my dignity.
Lost approximately 84 internal organs.
Would get fatality’d again.
amazing game , the best one in the last decade . 11 and 1 are pure bs compared to this masterpiece

Get the XL version, it gives you all of the dlc, the characters, the skins, it's a good deal.

This is probably the best Mortal Kombat game available on PC right now, so I would recommend it. It's got fast paced combat, whereas 11 feels a bit slower. Even just the towers and single fights against ai makes this worth it, and there are a ton of good characters, highly recommended even if you're not normally that into fighting games.

I’ve always loved the Mortal Kombat franchise, and MKX is honestly my GOAT fighting game. The gameplay feels fast, brutal, and super fun, and there’s a lot of variety in what you can do. Classic towers like back in the MK4 days, single fights, online multiplayer, different characters and variations… the game still feels great even after all these years.

What I really hate though is the way the studio pushes monetization and DLCs. The Krypt is packed with useless stuff like concept art and random unlocks instead of meaningful rewards. And the fact that you can now just unlock everything from the Steam store kind of ruins the whole point of grinding for it.

Still one of the best fighting games ever made for me.
